NKJV.1: My son, keep my words, And treasure my commands within you.
NKJV.2: Keep my commands and live,  And my law as the apple of your eye.
NKJV.3: Bind them on your fingers; Write them on the tablet of your heart.
NKJV.4: Say to wisdom, “You are my sister,” And call understanding your nearest kin,
NKJV.5: That they may keep you from the immoral woman, From the seductress who flatters with her words.
NKJV.6: For at the window of my house I looked through my lattice,
NKJV.7: And saw among the simple, I perceived among the youths, A young man devoid of understanding,
NKJV.8: Passing along the street near her corner; And he took the path to her house
NKJV.9: In the twilight, in the evening, In the black and dark night.
NKJV.10: And there a woman met him, With the attire of a harlot, and a crafty heart.
NKJV.11: She was loud and rebellious, Her feet would not stay at home.
NKJV.12: At times she was outside, at times in the open square, Lurking at every corner.
NKJV.13: So she caught him and kissed him; With an impudent face she said to him:
NKJV.14: “I have peace offerings with me; Today I have paid my vows.
NKJV.15: So I came out to meet you, Diligently to seek your face, And I have found you.
NKJV.16: I have spread my bed with tapestry, Colored coverings of Egyptian linen.
NKJV.17: I have perfumed my bed With myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon.
NKJV.18: Come, let us take our fill of love until morning; Let us delight ourselves with love.
NKJV.19: For my husband is not at home; He has gone on a long journey;
NKJV.20: He has taken a bag of money with him, And will come home on the appointed day.”
NKJV.21: With her enticing speech she caused him to yield,  With her flattering lips she seduced him.
NKJV.22: Immediately he went after her, as an ox goes to the slaughter, Or as a fool to the correction of the stocks,
NKJV.23: Till an arrow struck his liver. As a bird hastens to the snare, He did not know it would cost his life.
NKJV.24: Now therefore, listen to me, my children; Pay attention to the words of my mouth:
NKJV.25: Do not let your heart turn aside to her ways, Do not stray into her paths;
NKJV.26: For she has cast down many wounded, And all who were slain by her were strong men.
NKJV.27: Her house is the way to hell, Descending to the chambers of death.

KJV.1: My son<S>1121</S>, keep<S>8104</S> my words<S>561</S>, and lay up<S>6845</S> my commandments<S>4687</S> with thee.
KJV.2: Keep<S>8104</S> my commandments<S>4687</S>, and live<S>2421</S>; and my law<S>8451</S> as the apple<S>380</S> of thine eye<S>5869</S>.
KJV.3: Bind<S>7194</S> them upon thy fingers<S>676</S>, write<S>3789</S> them upon the table<S>3871</S> of thine heart<S>3820</S>.
KJV.4: Say<S>559</S> unto wisdom<S>2451</S>, Thou art my sister<S>269</S>; and call<S>7121</S> understanding<S>998</S> thy kinswoman<S>4129</S>:

KJV.6: For at the window<S>2474</S> of my house<S>1004</S> I looked<S>8259</S> through my casement<S>822</S>,
KJV.7: And beheld<S>7200</S> among the simple ones<S>6612</S>, I discerned<S>995</S> among the youths<S>1121</S>, a young man<S>5288</S> void<S>2638</S> of understanding<S>3820</S>, <sup>the youths: Heb. the sons</sup>
KJV.8: Passing through<S>5674</S> the street<S>7784</S> near<S>681</S> her corner<S>6434</S>; and he went<S>6805</S> the way<S>1870</S> to her house<S>1004</S>,
KJV.9: In the twilight<S>5399</S>, in the evening<S>3117</S> <S>6153</S>, in the black<S>380</S> and dark<S>653</S> night<S>3915</S>: <sup>in the evening: Heb. in the evening of day</sup>
KJV.10: And, behold, there met<S>7125</S> him a woman<S>802</S> with the attire<S>7897</S> of an harlot<S>2181</S>, and subtil<S>5341</S> of heart<S>3820</S>.
KJV.11: (She is loud<S>1993</S> and stubborn<S>5637</S>; her feet<S>7272</S> abide<S>7931</S> not in her house<S>1004</S>:
KJV.12: Now<S>6471</S> is she without<S>2351</S>, now<S>6471</S> in the streets<S>7339</S>, and lieth in wait<S>693</S> at<S>681</S> every corner<S>6438</S>.)
KJV.13: So she caught<S>2388</S> him, and kissed<S>5401</S> him, and with an impudent<S>5810</S> face<S>6440</S> said<S>559</S> unto him, <sup>with: Heb. she strengthened her face and said</sup>
KJV.14: I have peace<S>8002</S> offerings<S>2077</S> with me; this day<S>3117</S> have I payed<S>7999</S> my vows<S>5088</S>. <sup>I have: Heb. Peace offerings are upon me</sup>
KJV.15: Therefore came I forth<S>3318</S> to meet<S>7125</S> thee, diligently to seek<S>7836</S> thy face<S>6440</S>, and I have found<S>4672</S> thee.
KJV.16: I have decked<S>7234</S> my bed<S>6210</S> with coverings<S>4765</S> of tapestry, with carved<S>2405</S> works, with fine linen<S>330</S> of Egypt<S>4714</S>.
KJV.17: I have perfumed<S>5130</S> my bed<S>4904</S> with myrrh<S>4753</S>, aloes<S>174</S>, and cinnamon<S>7076</S>.
KJV.18: Come<S>3212</S>, let us take our fill<S>7301</S> of love<S>1730</S> until the morning<S>1242</S>: let us solace<S>5965</S> ourselves with loves<S>159</S>.
KJV.19: For the goodman<S>376</S> is not at home<S>1004</S>, he is gone<S>1980</S> a long<S>7350</S> journey<S>1870</S>:
KJV.20: He hath taken<S>3947</S> a bag<S>6872</S> of money<S>3701</S> with him<S>3027</S>, and will come<S>935</S> home<S>1004</S> at the day<S>3117</S> appointed<S>3677</S>. <sup>with: Heb. in his hand</sup> <sup>the day: or, the new moon</sup>
KJV.21: With her much<S>7230</S> fair speech<S>3948</S> she caused him to yield<S>5186</S>, with the flattering<S>2506</S> of her lips<S>8193</S> she forced<S>5080</S> him.
KJV.22: He goeth<S>1980</S> after<S>310</S> her straightway<S>6597</S>, as an ox<S>7794</S> goeth<S>935</S> to the slaughter<S>2874</S>, or as a fool<S>191</S> to the correction<S>4148</S> of the stocks<S>5914</S>; <sup>straightway: Heb. suddenly</sup>
KJV.23: Till a dart<S>2671</S> strike<S>6398</S> through his liver<S>3516</S>; as a bird<S>6833</S> hasteth<S>4116</S> to the snare<S>6341</S>, and knoweth<S>3045</S> not that it is for his life<S>5315</S>.
KJV.24: Hearken<S>8085</S> unto me now therefore, O ye children<S>1121</S>, and attend<S>7181</S> to the words<S>561</S> of my mouth<S>6310</S>.
KJV.25: Let not thine heart<S>3820</S> decline<S>7847</S> to her ways<S>1870</S>, go not astray<S>8582</S> in her paths<S>5410</S>.
KJV.26: For she hath cast down<S>5307</S> many<S>7227</S> wounded<S>2491</S>: yea, many strong<S>6099</S> men have been slain<S>2026</S> by her.
KJV.27: Her house<S>1004</S> is the way<S>1870</S> to hell<S>7585</S>, going down<S>3381</S> to the chambers<S>2315</S> of death<S>4194</S>.

ESV.1: My son, keep my words and treasure up my commandments with you;
ESV.2: keep my commandments and live; keep my teaching as the apple of your eye;
ESV.3: bind them on your fingers; write them on the tablet of your heart.
ESV.4: Say to wisdom, “You are my sister,” and call insight your intimate friend,
ESV.5: to keep you from the forbidden  woman, from the adulteress  with her smooth words.
ESV.6: For at the window of my house I have looked out through my lattice,
ESV.7: and I have seen among the simple, I have perceived among the youths, a young man lacking sense,
ESV.8: passing along the street near her corner, taking the road to her house
ESV.9: in the twilight, in the evening, at the time of night and darkness.
ESV.10: And behold, the woman meets him, dressed as a prostitute, wily of heart.
ESV.11: She is loud and wayward; her feet do not stay at home;
ESV.12: now in the street, now in the market, and at every corner she lies in wait.
ESV.13: She seizes him and kisses him, and with bold face she says to him,
ESV.14: “I had to offer sacrifices,  and today I have paid my vows;
ESV.15: so now I have come out to meet you, to seek you eagerly, and I have found you.
ESV.16: I have spread my couch with coverings, colored linens from Egyptian linen;
ESV.17: I have perfumed my bed with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon.
ESV.18: Come, let us take our fill of love till morning; let us delight ourselves with love.
ESV.19: For my husband is not at home; he has gone on a long journey;
ESV.20: he took a bag of money with him; at full moon he will come home.”
ESV.21: With much seductive speech she persuades him; with her smooth talk she compels him.
ESV.22: All at once he follows her, as an ox goes to the slaughter, or as a stag is caught fast
ESV.23: till an arrow pierces its liver; as a bird rushes into a snare; he does not know that it will cost him his life.
ESV.24: And now, O sons, listen to me, and be attentive to the words of my mouth.
ESV.25: Let not your heart turn aside to her ways; do not stray into her paths,
ESV.26: for many a victim has she laid low, and all her slain are a mighty throng.
ESV.27: Her house is the way to Sheol, going down to the chambers of death.

NASB.1: My son, keep my words And treasure my commandments within you.
NASB.2: Keep my commandments and live, And my teaching as the apple of your eye.
NASB.3: Bind them on your fingers; Write them on the tablet of your heart.
NASB.4: Say to wisdom, 'You are my sister,' And call understanding [your] intimate friend;
NASB.5: That they may keep you from an adulteress, From the foreigner who flatters with her words.
NASB.6: For at the window of my house I looked out through my lattice,
NASB.7: And I saw among the naive, [And] discerned among the youths A young man lacking sense,
NASB.8: Passing through the street near her corner; And he takes the way to her house,
NASB.9: In the twilight, in the evening, In the middle of the night and [in] the darkness.
NASB.10: And behold, a woman [comes] to meet him, Dressed as a harlot and cunning of heart.
NASB.11: She is boisterous and rebellious, Her feet do not remain at home;
NASB.12: [She is] now in the streets, now in the squares, And lurks by every corner.
NASB.13: So she seizes him and kisses him And with a brazen face she says to him:
NASB.14: 'I was due to offer peace offerings; Today I have paid my vows.
NASB.15: 'Therefore I have come out to meet you, To seek your presence earnestly, and I have found you.
NASB.16: 'I have spread my couch with coverings, With colored linens of Egypt.
NASB.17: 'I have sprinkled my bed With myrrh, aloes and cinnamon.
NASB.18: 'Come, let us drink our fill of love until morning; Let us delight ourselves with caresses.
NASB.19: 'For my husband is not at home, He has gone on a long journey;
NASB.20: He has taken a bag of money with him, At the full moon he will come home.'
NASB.21: With her many persuasions she entices him; With her flattering lips she seduces him.
NASB.22: Suddenly he follows her As an ox goes to the slaughter, Or as [one in] fetters to the discipline of a fool,
NASB.23: Until an arrow pierces through his liver; As a bird hastens to the snare, So he does not know that it [will cost him] his life.
NASB.24: Now therefore, [my] sons, listen to me, And pay attention to the words of my mouth.
NASB.25: Do not let your heart turn aside to her ways, Do not stray into her paths.
NASB.26: For many are the victims she has cast down, And numerous are all her slain.
NASB.27: Her house is the way to Sheol, Descending to the chambers of death.

NLT.1:
Follow my advice, my son;
always treasure my commands.
NLT.2:
Obey my commands and live!
Guard my instructions as you guard your own eyes.
NLT.3:
Tie them on your fingers as a reminder.
Write them deep within your heart.
NLT.4:
Love wisdom like a sister;
make insight a beloved member of your family.
NLT.5:
Let them protect you from an affair with an immoral woman,
from listening to the flattery of a promiscuous woman.
NLT.6:
While I was at the window of my house,
looking through the curtain,
NLT.7:
I saw some naive young men,
and one in particular who lacked common sense.
NLT.8:
He was crossing the street near the house of an immoral woman,
strolling down the path by her house.
NLT.9:
It was at twilight, in the evening,
as deep darkness fell.
NLT.10:
The woman approached him,
seductively dressed and sly of heart.
NLT.11:
She was the brash, rebellious type,
never content to stay at home.
NLT.12:
She is often in the streets and markets,
soliciting at every corner.
NLT.13:
She threw her arms around him and kissed him,
and with a brazen look she said,
NLT.14:
“I’ve just made my peace offerings
and fulfilled my vows.
NLT.15:
You’re the one I was looking for!
I came out to find you, and here you are!
NLT.16:
My bed is spread with beautiful blankets,
with colored sheets of Egyptian linen.
NLT.17:
I’ve perfumed my bed
with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon.
NLT.18:
Come, let’s drink our fill of love until morning.
Let’s enjoy each other’s caresses,
NLT.19:
for my husband is not home.
He’s away on a long trip.
NLT.20:
He has taken a wallet full of money with him
and won’t return until later this month. ”
NLT.21:
So she seduced him with her pretty speech
and enticed him with her flattery.
NLT.22:
He followed her at once,
like an ox going to the slaughter.
He was like a stag caught in a trap,
NLT.23:
awaiting the arrow that would pierce its heart.
He was like a bird flying into a snare,
little knowing it would cost him his life.
NLT.24:
So listen to me, my sons,
and pay attention to my words.
NLT.25:
Don’t let your hearts stray away toward her.
Don’t wander down her wayward path.
NLT.26:
For she has been the ruin of many;
many men have been her victims.
NLT.27:
Her house is the road to the grave.
Her bedroom is the den of death.

WEB.1: My son, keep my words. Lay up my commandments within you.
WEB.2: Keep my commandments and live! Guard my teaching as the apple of your eye.
WEB.3: Bind them on your fingers. Write them on the tablet of your heart.
WEB.4: Tell wisdom, “You are my sister.” Call understanding your relative,
WEB.5: that they may keep you from the strange woman, from the foreigner who flatters with her words.
WEB.6: For at the window of my house, I looked out through my lattice.
WEB.7: I saw among the simple ones. I discerned among the youths a young man void of understanding,
WEB.8: passing through the street near her corner, he went the way to her house,
WEB.9: in the twilight, in the evening of the day, in the middle of the night and in the darkness.
WEB.10: Behold, there a woman met him with the attire of a prostitute, and with crafty intent.
WEB.11: She is loud and defiant. Her feet don’t stay in her house.
WEB.12: Now she is in the streets, now in the squares, and lurking at every corner.
WEB.13: So she caught him, and kissed him. With an impudent face she said to him:
WEB.14: “Sacrifices of peace offerings are with me. Today I have paid my vows.
WEB.15: Therefore I came out to meet you, to diligently seek your face, and I have found you.
WEB.16: I have spread my couch with carpets of tapestry, with striped cloths of the yarn of Egypt.
WEB.17: I have perfumed my bed with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon.
WEB.18: Come, let’s take our fill of loving until the morning. Let’s solace ourselves with loving.
WEB.19: For my husband isn’t at home. He has gone on a long journey.
WEB.20: He has taken a bag of money with him. He will come home at the full moon.”
WEB.21: With persuasive words, she led him astray. With the flattering of her lips, she seduced him.
WEB.22: He followed her immediately, as an ox goes to the slaughter, as a fool stepping into a noose.
WEB.23: Until an arrow strikes through his liver, as a bird hurries to the snare, and doesn’t know that it will cost his life.
WEB.24: Now therefore, sons, listen to me. Pay attention to the words of my mouth.
WEB.25: Don’t let your heart turn to her ways. Don’t go astray in her paths,
WEB.26: for she has thrown down many wounded. Yes, all her slain are a mighty army.
WEB.27: Her house is the way to Sheol,  going down to the rooms of death.

CSB17.1:
My son, obey my words,
and treasure my commands. ⓓ
CSB17.2:
Keep my commands and live, ⓔ
and guard my instructions
as you would the pupil of your eye. ⓕ
CSB17.3:
Tie them to your fingers;
write them on the tablet of your heart. ⓖ
CSB17.4:
Say to wisdom, “You are my sister,” ⓗ
and call understanding your relative.
CSB17.5:
She will keep you from a forbidden woman,
a wayward woman with her flattering talk. ⓘ
CSB17.6:
At the window of my house
I looked through my lattice. ⓙ
CSB17.7:
I saw among the inexperienced, [1]
I noticed among the youths,
a young man lacking sense.
CSB17.8:
Crossing the street near her corner,
he strolled down the road to her house
CSB17.9:
at twilight, in the evening,
in the dark of the night. ⓚ
CSB17.10:
A woman came to meet him
dressed like a prostitute, ⓛ
having a hidden agenda. [2]
CSB17.11:
She is loud and defiant; ⓜ
her feet do not stay at home. ⓝ
CSB17.12:
Now in the street, now in the squares, ⓐ
she lurks at every corner. ⓑ
CSB17.13:
She grabs him and kisses him;
she brazenly says [3] to him,
CSB17.14:
“I’ve made fellowship offerings;
today I’ve fulfilled my vows. ⓒ
CSB17.15:
So I came out to meet you,
to search for you, and I’ve found you.
CSB17.16:
I’ve spread coverings on my bed ⓓ  —
richly colored linen from Egypt. ⓔ
CSB17.17:
I’ve perfumed my bed
with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon. ⓕ
CSB17.18:
Come, let’s drink deeply of lovemaking until morning. ⓖ
Let’s feast on each other’s love!
CSB17.19:
My husband isn’t home;
he went on a long journey.
CSB17.20:
He took a bag of silver with him
and will come home at the time of the full moon.”
CSB17.21:
She seduces him with her persistent pleading;
she lures with her flattering [4] talk.
CSB17.22:
He follows her impulsively
like an ox going to the slaughter,
like a deer bounding toward a trap [5]
CSB17.23:
until an arrow pierces its [6] liver, ⓗ
like a bird darting into a snare ⓘ  —
he doesn’t know it will cost him his life.
CSB17.24:
Now, sons, listen to me,
and pay attention to the words from my mouth.
CSB17.25:
Don’t let your heart turn aside to her ways;
don’t stray onto her paths. ⓙ
CSB17.26:
For she has brought many down to death;
her victims are countless. [7]
CSB17.27:
Her house is the road to Sheol,
descending to the chambers of death. ⓚ

AMP.1:
My son, keep my words
And treasure my commandments within you [so they are readily available to guide you].
AMP.2:
Keep my commandments and live,
And keep my teaching and law as the apple of your eye.
AMP.3:
Bind them [securely] on your fingers;
Write them on the tablet of your heart.
AMP.4:
Say to [skillful and godly] wisdom, "You are my sister,"
And regard understanding and intelligent insight as your intimate friends;
AMP.5:
That they may keep you from the immoral woman,
From the foreigner [who does not observe God's laws and] who flatters with her [smooth] words.
AMP.6:
For at the window of my house
I looked out through my lattice.
AMP.7:
And among the naive [the inexperienced and gullible],
I saw among the youths
A young man lacking [good] sense,
AMP.8:
Passing through the street near her corner;
And he took the path to her house
AMP.9:
In the twilight, in the evening;
In the black and dark night.
AMP.10:
And there a woman met him,
Dressed as a prostitute and sly and cunning of heart.
AMP.11:
She was boisterous and rebellious;
She would not stay at home.
AMP.12:
At times she was in the streets, at times in the market places,
Lurking and setting her ambush at every corner.
AMP.13:
So she caught him and kissed him
And with a brazen and impudent face she said to him:
AMP.14:
"I have peace offerings with me;
Today I have paid my vows.
AMP.15:
"So I came out to meet you [that you might share with me the feast of my offering],
Diligently I sought your face and I have found you.
AMP.16:
"I have spread my couch with coverings and cushions of tapestry,
With colored fine linen of Egypt.
AMP.17:
"I have perfumed my bed
With myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon.
AMP.18:
"Come, let us drink our fill of love until morning;
Let us console and delight ourselves with love.
AMP.19:
"For my husband is not at home.
He has gone on a long journey;
AMP.20:
He has taken a bag of money with him,
And he will come home on the appointed day."
AMP.21:
With her many persuasions she caused him to yield;
With her flattering lips she seduced him.
AMP.22:
Suddenly he went after her, as an ox goes to the slaughter [not knowing the outcome],
Or as one in stocks going to the correction [to be given] to a fool,
AMP.23:
Until an arrow pierced his liver [with a mortal wound];
Like a bird fluttering straight into the net,
He did not know that it would cost him his life.
AMP.24:
Now therefore, my sons, listen to me,
And pay attention to the words of my mouth.
AMP.25:
Do not let your heart turn aside to her ways,
Do not stray into her [evil, immoral] paths.
AMP.26:
For she has cast down many [mortally] wounded;
Indeed, all who were killed by her were strong.
AMP.27:
Her house is the way to Sheol,
Descending to the chambers of death.

MSG.1:
Dear friend, do what I tell you;
treasure my careful instructions.
MSG.2:
Do what I say and you'll live well.
My teaching is as precious as your eyesight—guard it!
MSG.3:
Write it out on the back of your hands;
etch it on the chambers of your heart.
MSG.4:
Talk to Wisdom as to a sister.
Treat Insight as your companion.
MSG.5:
They'll be with you to fend off the Temptress—
that smooth-talking, honey-tongued Seductress.
MSG.6:
As I stood at the window of my house
looking out through the shutters,
MSG.7:
Watching the mindless crowd stroll by,
I spotted a young man without any sense
MSG.8:
Arriving at the corner of the street where she lived,
then turning up the path to her house.
MSG.9:
It was dusk, the evening coming on,
the darkness thickening into night.
MSG.10:
Just then, a woman met him—
she'd been lying in wait for him, dressed to seduce him.
MSG.11:
Brazen and brash she was,
restless and roaming, never at home,
MSG.12:
Walking the streets, loitering in the mall,
hanging out at every corner in town.
MSG.13:
She threw her arms around him and kissed him,
boldly took his arm and said,
MSG.14:
"I've got all the makings for a feast—
today I made my offerings, my vows are all paid,
MSG.15:
So now I've come to find you,
hoping to catch sight of your face—and here you are!
MSG.16:
I've spread fresh, clean sheets on my bed,
colorful imported linens.
MSG.17:
My bed is aromatic with spices
and exotic fragrances.
MSG.18:
Come, let's make love all night,
spend the night in ecstatic lovemaking!
MSG.19:
My husband's not home; he's away on business,
and he won't be back for a month."
MSG.21:
Soon she has him eating out of her hand,
bewitched by her honeyed speech.
MSG.22:
Before you know it, he's trotting behind her,
like a calf led to the butcher shop,
Like a stag lured into ambush
MSG.23:
and then shot with an arrow,
Like a bird flying into a net
not knowing that its flying life is over.
MSG.24:
So, friends, listen to me,
take these words of mine most seriously.
MSG.25:
Don't fool around with a woman like that;
don't even stroll through her neighborhood.
MSG.26:
Countless victims come under her spell;
she's the death of many a poor man.
MSG.27:
She runs a halfway house to hell,
fits you out with a shroud and a coffin.

NIV.1:
Warning Against the Adulteress
My son, keep my words
and store up my commands within you.
NIV.2:
Keep my commands and you will live;
guard my teachings as the apple of your eye.
NIV.3:
Bind them on your fingers;
write them on the tablet of your heart.
NIV.4:
Say to wisdom, “You are my sister,”
and call understanding your kinsman;
NIV.5:
they will keep you from the adulteress,
from the wayward wife with her seductive words.
NIV.6:
At the window of my house
I looked out through the lattice.
NIV.7:
I saw among the simple,
I noticed among the young men,
a youth who lacked judgment.
NIV.8:
He was going down the street near her corner,
walking along in the direction of her house
NIV.9:
at twilight, as the day was fading,
as the dark of night set in.
NIV.10:
Then out came a woman to meet him,
dressed like a prostitute and with crafty intent.
NIV.11:
(She is loud and defiant,
her feet never stay at home;
NIV.12:
now in the street, now in the squares,
at every corner she lurks.)
NIV.13:
She took hold of him and kissed him
and with a brazen face she said:
NIV.14:
“I have fellowship offerings at home;
today I fulfilled my vows.
NIV.15:
So I came out to meet you;
I looked for you and have found you!
NIV.16:
I have covered my bed
with colored linens from Egypt.
NIV.17:
I have perfumed my bed
with myrrh, aloes and cinnamon.
NIV.18:
Come, let’s drink deep of love till morning;
let’s enjoy ourselves with love!
NIV.19:
My husband is not at home;
he has gone on a long journey.
NIV.20:
He took his purse filled with money
and will not be home till full moon.”
NIV.21:
With persuasive words she led him astray;
she seduced him with her smooth talk.
NIV.22:
All at once he followed her
like an ox going to the slaughter,
like a deer stepping into a noose
NIV.23:
till an arrow pierces his liver,
like a bird darting into a snare,
little knowing it will cost him his life.
NIV.24:
Now then, my sons, listen to me;
pay attention to what I say.
NIV.25:
Do not let your heart turn to her ways
or stray into her paths.
NIV.26:
Many are the victims she has brought down;
her slain are a mighty throng.
NIV.27:
Her house is a highway to the grave,
leading down to the chambers of death.

NRSVCE.1:
My child, keep my words
and store up my commandments with you;
NRSVCE.2:
keep my commandments and live,
keep my teachings as the apple of your eye;
NRSVCE.3:
bind them on your fingers,
write them on the tablet of your heart.
NRSVCE.4:
Say to wisdom, “You are my sister,”
and call insight your intimate friend,
NRSVCE.5:
that they may keep you from the loose woman,
from the adulteress with her smooth words.
NRSVCE.6:
For at the window of my house
I looked out through my lattice,
NRSVCE.7:
and I saw among the simple ones,
I observed among the youths,
a young man without sense,
NRSVCE.8:
passing along the street near her corner,
taking the road to her house
NRSVCE.9:
in the twilight, in the evening,
at the time of night and darkness.
NRSVCE.10:
Then a woman comes toward him,
decked out like a prostitute, wily of heart.
NRSVCE.11:
She is loud and wayward;
her feet do not stay at home;
NRSVCE.12:
now in the street, now in the squares,
and at every corner she lies in wait.
NRSVCE.13:
She seizes him and kisses him,
and with impudent face she says to him:
NRSVCE.14:
“I had to offer sacrifices,
and today I have paid my vows;
NRSVCE.15:
so now I have come out to meet you,
to seek you eagerly, and I have found you!
NRSVCE.16:
I have decked my couch with coverings,
colored spreads of Egyptian linen;
NRSVCE.17:
I have perfumed my bed with myrrh,
aloes, and cinnamon.
NRSVCE.18:
Come, let us take our fill of love until morning;
let us delight ourselves with love.
NRSVCE.19:
For my husband is not at home;
he has gone on a long journey.
NRSVCE.20:
He took a bag of money with him;
he will not come home until full moon.”
NRSVCE.21:
With much seductive speech she persuades him;
with her smooth talk she compels him.
NRSVCE.22:
Right away he follows her,
and goes like an ox to the slaughter,
or bounds like a stag toward the trap
NRSVCE.23:
until an arrow pierces its entrails.
He is like a bird rushing into a snare,
not knowing that it will cost him his life.
NRSVCE.24:
And now, my children, listen to me,
and be attentive to the words of my mouth.
NRSVCE.25:
Do not let your hearts turn aside to her ways;
do not stray into her paths.
NRSVCE.26:
For many are those she has laid low,
and numerous are her victims.
NRSVCE.27:
Her house is the way to Sheol,
going down to the chambers of death.

NET.1: My child, keep my words and treasure up my commands in your own keeping.
NET.2: Keep my commands so that you may live, and obey my instruction as your most prized possession.
NET.3: Bind them on your forearm; write them on the tablet of your heart.
NET.4: Say to wisdom, “You are my sister,” and call understanding a close relative,
NET.5: so that they may keep you from the adulterous woman, from the loose woman who flatters you with her words.
NET.6: For at the window of my house through my window lattice I looked out
NET.7: and I saw among the naive — I discerned among the youths — a young man who lacked wisdom.
NET.8: He was passing by the street near her corner, making his way along the road to her house
NET.9: in the twilight, the evening, in the dark of the night.
NET.10: Suddenly a woman came out to meet him! She was dressed like a prostitute and with secret intent.
NET.11: (She is loud and rebellious, she does not remain at home —
NET.12: at one time outside, at another in the wide plazas, and by every corner she lies in wait.)
NET.13: So she grabbed him and kissed him, and with a bold expression she said to him,
NET.14: “I have fresh meat at home; today I have fulfilled my vows!
NET.15: That is why I came out to meet you, to look for you, and I found you!
NET.16: I have spread my bed with elegant coverings, with richly colored fabric from Egypt.
NET.17: I have perfumed my bed with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon.
NET.18: Come, let’s drink deeply of lovemaking until morning, let’s delight ourselves with sexual intercourse.
NET.19: For my husband is not at home; he has gone on a journey of some distance.
NET.20: He has taken a bag of money with him; he will not return until the end of the month.”
NET.21: She persuaded him with persuasive words; with her smooth talk she compelled him.
NET.22: Suddenly he went after her like an ox that goes to the slaughter, like a stag prancing into a trapper’s snare
NET.23: till an arrow pierces his liver — like a bird hurrying into a trap, and he does not know that it will cost him his life.
NET.24: So now, sons, listen to me, and pay attention to the words I speak.
NET.25: Do not let your heart turn aside to her ways — do not wander into her pathways;
NET.26: for she has brought down many fatally wounded, and all those she has slain are many.
NET.27: Her house is the way to the grave, going down to the chambers of death.
